name: Chick Webb
abstract: William Henry "Chick" Webb (February 10, 1905 – June 16, 1939) was an American jazz and swing music drummer and band leader.

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Ascione
about_quotes.evidence: You have to hear Chick Webb. ... in the big band era you have to listen to Jo Jones and even Chick Webb and Sonny Greer and Gene Krupa.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Ascione interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 17, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Jones Hank
about_quotes.evidence: Ella, having come from a background of starting out with Chick Webb’s big band preferred sort of, the band, chordal accompaniment.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jones Hank — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Albert Nicholas
about_quotes.evidence: I got out of the band in 1933 for about six months or so and played with Chick Webb at the Savoy.

about_quotes.speaker: Roy Haynes
about_quotes.evidence: I had the record Liza by Chick Webb. ... I didn’t see Chick Webb.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roy Haynes — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Helen Oakley Dance
about_quotes.evidence: Well anyway, so that office hired me to make a hit with Chick... And so I built Chick up... I said to him, you know, 'Chick, if the office wants to fire me, let them. I’ve done my job, I’ve put you where you’re supposed to be and that’s what they hired me for.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Helen Oakley Dance interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 12, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Mario Bauza
about_quotes.evidence: this guy give me my first break... I feel better in Chick's band than I do in Duke's band... I come here, you know, with Duke Ellington... they listen to me play... the job is yours.
